LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED, NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT RAISED. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE MERELY DEMONSTRATES GENERAL RETENTION OF CONTROL OVER SAFETY CONDITIONS AT THE WORKSITE, BUT SUCH RETENTION IS INSUFFICIENT TO IMPOSE LIABILITY ON DEFENDANT. (SEE HOOKER V. DEPT. OF TRANSPORTATION (2002) 27 CAL.4TH 198; MADDEN V. SUMMIT VIEW, INC. (2008) 165 CAL APP 4TH 1267.) NO EVIDENCE OF AFFIRMATIVE CONDUCT ON DEFENDANT'S BEHALF. NO EVIDENCE OF AN ACTIONABLE OMISSION BY DEFENDANT AS DESCRIBED IN HOOKER. NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT AFFIRMATIVELY CONTRIBUTED TO ANY DANGER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (ORDER RE: TURNER CONSTRUCTION'S OBJECTION TO EVID. FILED IN OPEN COURT AND COPIES GIVEN TO COUNSEL AT TODAY'S HEARING.) ORDER REGARDING TODAY'S RULING SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: JOSEPH VICKSTEIN, CSR #4780

DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR COSTS AND FEES FOR UNJUSTIFIED DENIAL OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAD REASONABLE GROUNDS TO BELIEVE THAT SHE WOULD PREVAIL ON THE MATTERS DENIED AND FURTHER FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAD OTHER GOOD REASON FOR THE FAILURE TO ADMIT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT. NOT REPORTED.

Re-Notice By Stip Of Motion For Costs And Fees For Injustified Denial Of Request For Admission
Off Calendar May-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY's Motion For Costs And Fees For Unjustified Denial Of Requests For Admission is denied. The court finds that Plaintiff had reasonable grounds to believe that she would prevail on the matters denied and further finds that Plaintiff had other good reason for the failure to admit. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Ntc And Motion For Costs And Fees For Unjustified Denial Of Requests For Admissionn
Off Calendar Apr-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY Motion for Costs and Fees for Unjustified Denial of Requests for Admission. Appearances: Bruce E. Weisenberg, of Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer (415) 397-2800 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. The Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar, discovery motion. Please call and re-notice motion in the discovery department. (302/EHG) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR# 7489

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-06-2010 Continued To Aug-09-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-06-2010 Continued To Jul-28-10 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 505

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Matthew N. White, Attorney at Law, (415) 453-1010 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party;
William A. Bogdan, Esq., Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer (415) 397-2800 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment is granted, no triable issue of material fact raised. Plaintiff's evidence merely demonstrates general retention of control over safety conditions at the worksite, but such retention is insufficient to impose liability on defendant. (See Hooker v. Dept. of Transportation (2002) 27 Cal.4th 198; Madden v. Summit View, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal App 4th 1267.) No evidence of affirmative conduct on defendant's behalf. No evidence of an actionable omission by defendant as described in Hooker. No evidence that defendant affirmatively contributed to any danger. Order signed in open Court. (Order re: Turner construction's objection to Evid. Filed in Open Court and copies given to counsel at today's hearing.) Order regarding today's ruling signed in open Court. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Joseph Vickstein, CSR # 4780

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-06-2010 Text Ruling

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
